arguments.task: Open official Pinterest developer documentation pages from developers.pinterest.com and related official Pinterest business/help pages as needed. Determine: 1) one-line API description, 2) every documented authentication method for the Pinterest API, 3) whether API access is self-serve free, self-serve trial, paid plan required, admin approval required, or partner/contact-sales gated, with exact evidence, 4) whether the API surface is REST, GraphQL, Both, or None and summarize breadth of endpoints/products, 5) whether an official or existing MCP server/integration is documented, 6) rate limit notes. Only use live official first-party pages that fully load and return content. Read the pages themselves; do not rely on search snippets. Return a concise structured summary with exact page URLs used as evidence.

input.thought: Start browser-based review of official Pinterest docs and supporting first-party pages.

Sandboxed content (visible only to creator).\\n   - **Standard Access**: Requires addition...[truncated]

raw_result.description: Pinterest’s API lets developers build apps for conversion tracking, content and board management, ads, shopping, and analytics/reporting.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official auth docs describe OAuth 2.0 flows/grants including authorization code, client credentials, and refresh token usage; no separate API key, personal access token, or HTTP Basic method was evidenced.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official access tiers docs state Trial access is granted upon approval, and Standard access requires additional review including a demo video of the OAuth flow and app usage.

api_surface.breadth: Pinterest API v5 documentation covers endpoints for organic content and boards plus business/ads capabilities including ad accounts, campaigns, audiences/targeting, catalogs/shopping, conversions, billing, business access, and analytics/reporting.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP or Model Context Protocol server was evidenced in the official docs reviewed; only API/SDK documentation was found.

raw_result.blocker: A first toolkit is blocked today because Pinterest API access requires manual approval/review rather than self-serve developer activation.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate limit docs say Trial access is generally 1,000 requests/day per app, while Standard access is generally 100 requests/second per user per app; some categories such as ads conversions have higher category-specific limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: Pinterest publishes a current v5 API reference, explicit OAuth setup docs, explicit access-tier review requirements, and granular rate-limit documentation—strong signs of a mature but access-controlled platform.
